businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker transitioning into a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning robotaxis, humanoid robots, and energy storage
businessSummary.description: Tesla is rapidly transforming from an EV manufacturer into an integrated AI, robotics, and energy platform company. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B, marking its first-ever annual revenue decline (-2.9%), with net income plunging 46.5%. Closed at $396.73 (-2.17%) on 3/6, pummeled by a disastrous jobs report and oil surging to $90+ amid the Iran war — Wall Street's worst week since October. UK Feb sales crashed -37% (2,422 units), Toyota and Stellantis left Tesla's EU CO2 pool. NHTSA FSD deadline is D-2 (3/9). P/E 368.71x, Forward P/E 150.88x.
howTheyMakeMoney: Vehicle sales: ~73% of revenue ($69.5B). Model 3/Y core, Model S/X discontinuing Q2 2026
Energy business: ~14% of revenue ($12.8B, +27% YoY). Megapack utility-scale storage
Services/Other: ~13% of revenue ($12.5B). Supercharger network, insurance, maintenance
FSD subscription: $99/month autonomous driving software (subscription-only since Feb 2026)
keyProducts: Cybertruck (base AWD trim with massive demand, deliveries pushed to 2027)
Optimus (humanoid robot, Fremont factory conversion underway)
Megapack (utility-scale energy storage, Megapack 3 and Mega Block launching 2026)

keyMetrics.name: P/E Ratio (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme valuation. AI/robotics premium vs actual earnings

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Reflects earnings recovery expectations but still very elevated

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: First-ever FY decline. Global EV competition intensifying

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Slight improvement QoQ but still historically low

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down from FY2024. Profitability recovery needed

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA FSD Data Deadline D-2 — March 9 Regulatory Inflection
growthDrivers.description: FSD crash data submission deadline is 3/9. Approval could accelerate autonomous driving rollout; failure risks 30% downside (Nasdaq analysis).

growthDrivers.title: UK Feb Sales -37%, Toyota & Stellantis Leave EU CO2 Pool
growthDrivers.description: UK Feb deliveries crashed to 2,422 units (-37% YoY) while overall market grew 7.2%. Toyota and Stellantis exiting Tesla's EU emissions pool further erodes regulatory credit revenue.

growthDrivers.title: BofA Maintains Buy $460 — 'Current Leader in Consumer Autonomy'
growthDrivers.description: BofA Securities resumed Buy coverage with $460 target on 3/4, citing Tesla's autonomous driving leadership.

growthDrivers.title: Cybertruck Base AWD Trim — Explosive Demand, Deliveries Pushed to 2027
growthDrivers.description: New base trim generating massive demand, pushing delivery estimates into 2027.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share leader, though overtaken globally by BYD. Vertical integration across software, manufacturing, energy, and 8.2B FSD miles is the core moat.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles. Overwhelming training data advantage over competitors.
In-house battery (4680), custom chips (HW4), software (FSD), charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS is North America's EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian all depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: 2025 global EV sales leader. Rapid European expansion, US limited by tariffs

competitors.name: Rivian (RIVN)
competitors.detail: R2 SUV ($45,000) launching 2026, directly competing with Model Y

competitors.name: Xiaomi
competitors.detail: YU7 SUV outselling Model Y 2:1 in China

recentNews.headline: TSLA -2.17% as jobs shock + oil surge to $90+ trigger Wall Street's worst week since October
recentNews.significance: Nonfarm payrolls contracted unexpectedly while Iran war pushed oil to 2023 highs. Broad risk-off selling

recentNews.headline: UK February sales plunge 37% to 2,422 units while overall market grows 7.2%
recentNews.significance: BYD +40.9% in contrast. European demand erosion deepening

recentNews.headline: Musk's X deal trial rekindles legal risks for Tesla
recentNews.significance: Ongoing CEO risk as legal and political controversies continue to weigh on brand sentiment

recentNews.headline: Toyota and Stellantis exit Tesla's EU CO2 emissions pool
recentNews.significance: Largest participants leaving further erodes regulatory credit revenue

recentNews.headline: BNP Paribas cuts TSLA target to $280 from $313
recentNews.significance: European sales weakness and valuation concerns reflected in downgrade

analystOpinions.notableComment: 45 analysts, Hold consensus. Target $426.61 implies +7.5% upside. BofA Buy $460, BNP $280 — extreme divergence. 3/9 NHTSA outcome is the next catalyst for target revisions.

risks.title: P/E 369x extreme valuation
risks.description: Massively overvalued vs current earnings. AI/robotics narrative failure risks severe correction.

risks.title: European demand collapse — UK -37%, EU Jan registrations -17%
risks.description: UK, Netherlands, Denmark sharp declines. Toyota/Stellantis CO2 pool exit shrinks credit revenue.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD regulatory risk (D-2)
risks.description: 3/9 data submission could result in FSD program suspension. Failure = 30% downside.

risks.title: Macro headwinds — oil $90+, jobs shock
risks.description: Iran war + employment contraction could dampen consumer sentiment and EV purchases.

risks.title: CEO risk — X deal trial + political controversies
risks.description: Musk's legal and political issues continue to drive consumer backlash and brand damage.

buyReasons.title: 7.5% discount to consensus $427
buyReasons.rationale: Dropped from $405 to $397 on macro selloff. Could be technical overshoot.

buyReasons.title: NHTSA 3/9 pass could trigger rally
buyReasons.rationale: FSD approval would unlock autonomous driving valuation re-rating.

buyReasons.title: FCF $6.22B rebound (+73.7% YoY)
buyReasons.rationale: Cash generation improving despite revenue decline. Investment capacity secured.

buyReasons.title: Energy business high growth + ESG #1
buyReasons.rationale: Revenue $12.8B (+27% YoY). #1 supply chain sustainability attracts institutional capital.

overallOpinion: Jobs shock and oil surging to $90+ drove TSLA down -2.17% to $396.73, capping Wall Street's worst week since October.
UK Feb sales -37%, Toyota/Stellantis EU CO2 pool exit worsening both European demand and regulatory credit revenue outlook.
NHTSA FSD data deadline is two days away (3/9). Binary event: pass triggers rally, fail risks 30% downside.
Clinging to the 200-day SMA at $392, below the 50-day at $428. RSI 41 approaching oversold. March 9 determines near-term direction.

The real D-day is 3/9 NHTSA FSD deadline. Pass = rally, fail = 30% downside. Weekend to breathe, Monday decides everything. investory.kro.kr/stock/TSLA/analysis/2026-03-07
